records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Circumstantiated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Circumstantiating (?).]

defs: To place in particular circumstances; to invest with particular accidents or adjuncts.
quotes: If the act were otherwise circumstantiated, it might will that freely which now it wills reluctantly.

defs: To prove or confirm by circumstances; to enter into details concerning.
quotes: Neither will time permint to circumstantiate these particulars, which I have only touched in the general.
